2026-04-07T22:42:19.3161983Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-07T22:42:19.3162429Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-07T22:42:19.3162816Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-07T22:42:19.3163131Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-07T22:43:08.6370934Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T22:42:54.516054074Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57896,\"title\":\"Intermittent test failure: SessionProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken due to CancelAfter(500) race condition\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nThe unit test `SessionProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken` in `Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Tests` is intermittently failing in CI with a `TaskCanceledException`.\\n\\n**Example failure:** [Build 6120413, log 2784](https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/29ec6040-b234-4e31-b139-33dc4287b756/_apis/build/builds/6120413/logs/2784)\\n\\n## Root Cause Analysis\\n\\nThe test creates a `CancellationTokenSource` with `CancelAfter(500)` and passes its token through the `CloseAsync` call chain. The intent is **not** to test cancellation behavior — the comment in the code says:\\n\\n\\u0026gt; *\\u0026#34;mutate the cancellation token to distinguish it from CancellationToken.None\\u0026#34;*\\n\\nThe `CancelAfter(500)` is purely to make the token identity-distinguishable from `default(CancellationToken)` so that the `Verify` call can confirm it was passed through correctly.\\n\\nHowever, because `CallBase = true`, the **real** `StopProcessingAsync` executes, which calls `_processingStartStopSemaphore.WaitAsync(cancellationToken)` at line 751 of `ServiceBusProcessor.cs`. If 500ms elapses before reaching that line, the already-cancelled token causes `SemaphoreSlim.WaitAsync` to throw `TaskCanceledException`.\\n\\n### Why it\\u0026#39;s intermittent\\n\\nThe call chain traverses **two layers of Moq proxies** (`Mock` → `Mock`). On fast machines this completes in microseconds. On loaded CI runners (this macOS runner was executing 7+ test assemblies in parallel), the chain can be delayed past 500ms. The failing run took **874ms** — well past the 500ms timer.\\n\\n### Evidence\\n\\nFrom the CI logs:\\n- `Passed CloseRespectsCancellationToken [6 ms]` — non-session `ServiceBusProcessor` version (1 Moq layer, fast)\\n- `Failed CloseRespectsCancellationToken [874 ms]` — session processor version (2 Moq layers, slow under load)\\n\\nThe same latent bug also exists in `ProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken` (line 490), which uses the identical `CancelAfter(500)` pattern — it just hasn\\u0026#39;t failed yet because the single-layer call chain is faster.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nRemove `CancelAfter(500)` from both tests. A token from `new CancellationTokenSource()` (without `CancelAfter`) is already identity-distinguishable from `CancellationToken.None` — `CancellationToken` equality compares the backing source reference, and a real source is never equal to `default`. This eliminates the race entirely while preserving the test\\u0026#39;s verification logic.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57896\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Service Bus\",\"Client\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-07T22:40:23Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-07T22:40:23Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-07T22:43:08.6383144Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T22:42:54.528920073Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57896,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-07T22:43:08.6397273Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T22:42:54.732744958Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57896,\"title\":\"Intermittent test failure: SessionProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken due to CancelAfter(500) race condition\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nThe unit test `SessionProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken` in `Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Tests` is intermittently failing in CI with a `TaskCanceledException`.\\n\\n**Example failure:** [Build 6120413, log 2784](https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/29ec6040-b234-4e31-b139-33dc4287b756/_apis/build/builds/6120413/logs/2784)\\n\\n## Root Cause Analysis\\n\\nThe test creates a `CancellationTokenSource` with `CancelAfter(500)` and passes its token through the `CloseAsync` call chain. The intent is **not** to test cancellation behavior — the comment in the code says:\\n\\n\\u0026gt; *\\u0026#34;mutate the cancellation token to distinguish it from CancellationToken.None\\u0026#34;*\\n\\nThe `CancelAfter(500)` is purely to make the token identity-distinguishable from `default(CancellationToken)` so that the `Verify` call can confirm it was passed through correctly.\\n\\nHowever, because `CallBase = true`, the **real** `StopProcessingAsync` executes, which calls `_processingStartStopSemaphore.WaitAsync(cancellationToken)` at line 751 of `ServiceBusProcessor.cs`. If 500ms elapses before reaching that line, the already-cancelled token causes `SemaphoreSlim.WaitAsync` to throw `TaskCanceledException`.\\n\\n### Why it\\u0026#39;s intermittent\\n\\nThe call chain traverses **two layers of Moq proxies** (`Mock` → `Mock`). On fast machines this completes in microseconds. On loaded CI runners (this macOS runner was executing 7+ test assemblies in parallel), the chain can be delayed past 500ms. The failing run took **874ms** — well past the 500ms timer.\\n\\n### Evidence\\n\\nFrom the CI logs:\\n- `Passed CloseRespectsCancellationToken [6 ms]` — non-session `ServiceBusProcessor` version (1 Moq layer, fast)\\n- `Failed CloseRespectsCancellationToken [874 ms]` — session processor version (2 Moq layers, slow under load)\\n\\nThe same latent bug also exists in `ProcessorTests.CloseRespectsCancellationToken` (line 490), which uses the identical `CancelAfter(500)` pattern — it just hasn\\u0026#39;t failed yet because the single-layer call chain is faster.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nRemove `CancelAfter(500)` from both tests. A token from `new CancellationTokenSource()` (without `CancelAfter`) is already identity-distinguishable from `CancellationToken.None` — `CancellationToken` equality compares the backing source reference, and a real source is never equal to `default`. This eliminates the race entirely while preserving the test\\u0026#39;s verification logic.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57896\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Service Bus\",\"Client\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-07T22:40:23Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-07T22:40:23Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
